page: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated CPU virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The server is configured with 31.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 3.91 GB of RAM per core, and includes 240 GB of local SSD storage. Virtualization is managed via the QEMU hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the ccx33 demonstrates strong single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 3061.27 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 13.79 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Text processing performance is also strong, with a Geekbench text processing score of 2431.0. Most other compute and memory metrics fall within the average range, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1871.0 and a multi-core score of 7266.0. However, the system exhibits a bottleneck in large-block memory bandwidth, where uncached read performance drops to 38.5 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25%. Similarly, prompt processing for medium-sized 7B language models is weak at 22.49 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 9.31 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core instances, representing a stable hardware profile. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance well-suited for general-purpose workloads. Recommended applications include static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate text-processing tasks. It is less optimal for memory-bound applications requiring high uncached read bandwidth or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 31.0 GB of RAM and 240 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database execution speeds. However, it exhibits lower performance in large-block uncached memory read bandwidth and medium-scale LLM prompt processing. Given its dedicated CPU resources and balanced hardware profile, the ccx33 is highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations where consistent CPU performance is required without resource contention.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 240 GB of local SSD storage, delivering stable performance for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 240 GB of local SSD storage.
tagline: A dedicated virtual server offering balanced compute and memory resources with dedicated AMD EPYC cores for predictable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs with 4 physical cores and hyperthreading
31.0 GB of system memory and 240 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Average multi-core, database, and web serving capabilities
Weak large-block uncached memory read bandwidth
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and databases
